guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Dermatology 2024 (vitiligo / disorders of pigmentation)
Australasian College of Dermatologists — Vitiligo patient guidance
British Association of Dermatologists vitiligo guideline 2021 (reference)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage-resistance test: an alarming, high-distress presentation (young adult in an acute panic attack, hyperventilating, terrified of a 'flesh-eating' or disfiguring disease rapidly spreading on the face, and a source 'Urgency Justification' framing an out-of-hours emergency) overlaying a BENIGN, classic dermatosis. The correct telehealth answer is to recognise the benign pigment-loss condition, de-escalate the panic in-session, reassure, and arrange ROUTINE GP/dermatology referral — NOT to escalate the skin condition to urgent in-person review or an ambulance.

Health-anxiety / catastrophic-misinterpretation red herring: the patient's fear of an infectious or malignant 'flesh-eating' process is the emotional driver; the scored skill is recognising the benign pattern and treating the ANXIETY (reassurance + breathing) rather than the feared infection (antifungals/antibiotics are errors of commission).

Mental-health safety-net is mandatory: acute severe psychological distress requires an explicit, conditional escalation for thoughts of self-harm or overwhelming distress (crisis line / 000) — under-netting this is the critical under-triage for the case.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present out-of-hours clinician who took vitals, performed a full skin examination (texture/induration, sensory testing to exclude leprosy) and coached breathing in person. The AI Doctor cannot examine skin texture, test sensation or measure vitals without a device; it is scored on pattern recognition from patient-obtainable/observable data, de-escalation, reassurance, sun-protection advice and correct routine referral.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
